title: Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation for Whole Mouse Brain After morphoFISH Perfusion
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9vsjm","text":"This is a slightly modified version of Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation to optimize RNA retention following morphoFISH perfusion. Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation was the default delipidation protocol used by the MouseLight Project Team since ca. 2018, and since then disseminated between MouseLight collaborators in secondary reports, e.g.:  Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation of a Whole Mouse Brain and 'Uniclear' water-based brain clearing for light sheet imaging of electrode tracks. The protocol was first proposed by Zhuhao Wu.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":333,"length":50},{"key":1,"offset":388,"length":81}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"00EFE467826811EFA3260A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.n2bvj81mwgk5/v1"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"F6A8588BFEF211EF89CA0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zndf5a6"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ajck","text":"It is recommended to etch animal identification information on the glass vial. Ink or label adhesive may dissolve if exposed to organic solvents.  Alternatively ink jet printed label can be place on glass vial covered with a piece of Scotch Magic tap (cat #810) to protect ink from dissolving.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Use a 20 mL vial for processing an adult mouse brain. All steps in this section are carried out on a carousel rotator 10 rpm, Room temperature, with samples protected from light. Replace solution in vial with 20 mL SBiP for each of the following steps: SBiP for 01:00:00 SBiP for 03:00:00 SBiP Overnight SBiP for 24:00:00 SBiP for 24:00:00 SBiP for 24:00:00 SBiP for 24:00:00 It is important that the SBiP solution is mixed thoroughly during the aqueous steps in order to effectively delipidate the brain. When it is thoroughly mixed, the solution should look uniformly turbid at Room temperature . 2-Methyl-2-butanol and 2-propanol are corrosive and flammable. Perform the steps that involve these reagents under the fume hood. Wear a lab coat, safety goggles or glasses, and gloves.
Wash the brain with 20 mL B1n buffer at Room temperature Overnight .
Wash the brain with 1X PBS, rotating at Room temperature for the following steps: PBS for 01:00:00 PBS for 01:00:00, +
Delipidate with aqueous SBiP solution Start with a whole mouse brain perfused via morphoFISH perfusion protocol stored in 1x PBS in a 20 mL vial.
Aqueous (SBiP) delipidation complete. It is recommended to proceed to imaging as soon as possible to minimize RNA loss prior to detection.

Monitoring lipid extraction is useful to gauge delipidaton progress. While monitoring the extraction of total lipids in the sample is challenging, monitoring unsaturated fatty acids (UFA) in the tissue is easily achievable using fluoremetric variants of the sulfo-phospho-vanillin (SPV) method. In turn, UFA detection can be used as a bona fide surrogate of total lipids. Here is an example of an extraction curve obtained using such a quantification strategy (CellBioLabs lipid-quantification-kit, # STA-613). Example of an extraction curve obtained using fluorometric SPV. Measurements were performed in controlled volumes (16mL/incubation), with an estimated sensitivity of ~39 ng/mL (~600 ng). N=7, adult females, isogenic C57BL/6J strain. Values normalized to blank controls and day 0 values.
creator: Monique Copeland
